actionUnits.condition: Scene confirms MonoBehaviour_0 (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is the Fire script attached to Fire-Wrapper - R - 1 - 1. This instance has a BoxCollider (no Rigidbody). Goal: validate Fire turning OFF via water interaction. Fire.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) resets currentHitInterval and accumulates currentHitTime; if hits continue within maxHitInterval (2s) until totalHitTime (5s), fire should turn off and UpdateFireState(true) is called. However, WaterHit requires a Vector3 parameter and there are no UnityEvent m_Calls exposed in the scene for parameterless triggering. We need either a water emitter object that will call WaterHit via collision/interaction, or a parameterless proxy method to invoke WaterHit for testing.

Validate Fire auto ignition via neighbors. In this instance, neighbors: [] and onNeighborsThreshold: 2. Fire should start turning on (shouldTurnOn = true) only when onNeighbors >= 2. timeToOn is randomized in Awake between timeToOnMin (40) and timeToOnMax (60), and accelerates if a down neighbor exists. To execute this test, we need neighbors to be populated (FindNeighbors called by controller or neighbors preassigned in scene) and at least two neighbor fires set to isOn = true. Without neighbors provided in the scene meta and with no parameterless public methods to call, this event will not occur automatically.
